Good Health and Well-being.reason: Examines historical perspectives on health through '早期古文字中的疾病與醫療' (Disease and medicine in early ancient characters), a tangential link to understanding historical health risks (Target 3.d).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and promotes appreciation of cultural heritage and critical thinking, key aspects of Target 4.7.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Develops '專業能力' (Professional Competence) and '思辨與創新能力' (Speculation and Innovation Capacity), which are foundational skills for creativity and innovation mentioned in Target 8.3.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Analyzes the evolution of writing as a technology, from ancient scripts to '網路語言' (internet language), linking to historical and modern innovation (Target 9.5).

Climate Action.reason: Touches upon historical climate awareness through the study of '氣象卜辭' (meteorological divination inscriptions), a very minimal link to Target 13.3.

Life on Land.reason: Examines historical human-nature interaction by studying ancient characters for '山河' (mountains and rivers) and '動物' (animals), relating to cultural understanding of ecosystems (Target 15.1).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Explores the relationship between writing, power, and law ('擁有文字等於擁有權力？') through historical texts, relating to the development of institutions and justice (Targets 16.3, 16.6).
